# Quarterly Earnings Report — Q1 2026

**Company:** ExampleCorp (NASDAQ: EXMP)  
**Filing date:** April 28, 2026  
**Period covered:** January 1 – March 31, 2026

## Forward-looking statements

This report contains forward-looking statements within the meaning of
Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933 and Section 21E of the Securities
Exchange Act of 1934. These statements are based on current expectations
and assumptions, and actual results may differ materially.

## Financial highlights

| Metric           | Q1 2026  | Q1 2025  | Change  |
| ---------------- | -------- | -------- | ------- |
| Revenue          | $847.2 M | $712.6 M | +18.9 % |
| Gross margin     | 72.4 %   | 70.1 %   | +2.3 pp |
| Operating income | $156.3 M | $98.4 M  | +58.8 % |
| Net income       | $124.7 M | $76.2 M  | +63.7 % |
| Diluted EPS      | $1.42    | $0.88    | +61.4 % |
| Free cash flow   | $203.5 M | $141.2 M | +44.1 % |

## Revenue breakdown

Revenue growth was driven primarily by enterprise subscription revenue,
which increased 24 % year-over-year to $612.3 M. Professional services
revenue grew 8 % to $145.8 M. The remaining $89.1 M came from training
and certification programs.

## Operating expenses

Total operating expenses were $456.7 M, up 12 % from Q1 2025. The increase
reflects headcount growth in R&D (+18 % YoY) and sales (+15 % YoY), partially
offset by a 5 % reduction in general and administrative costs.

## Balance sheet highlights

As of March 31, 2026, ExampleCorp held $2.34 B in cash and marketable
securities, with $1.18 B in long-term debt. The current ratio stands at
2.8:1, compared to 2.4:1 a year ago.

## Risk factors

Our results remain subject to foreign exchange volatility (approximately
32 % of revenue is non-USD), customer concentration risk (top 10 customers
account for 18 % of revenue), and ongoing regulatory uncertainty in AI
and data privacy compliance across multiple jurisdictions.

## Guidance

For Q2 2026, we expect revenue between $870 M and $890 M, representing
20–23 % year-over-year growth. Full-year 2026 revenue guidance is raised
to $3.50 B–$3.60 B, up from previous guidance of $3.35 B–$3.50 B.

This guidance assumes no material change in macroeconomic conditions,
exchange rates, or regulatory requirements.

_This document is a simplified illustration. It does not constitute
investment advice or a complete regulatory filing._
